programme.title: Amor perdido
programme.desc: A beautiful young girl named Amalia.(played by actress Amalia Aguilar). with a talent for Rumba, wants to become a glamorous cabaret singer. Her dream is encouraged by her best friend, Ernesto, (Tito Novaro), who also happens to be a gifted composer. While working towards her career goal, Amalia meets the ultimate "bad-boy", a dashing gangster named Luis, (Victor Junco). She is quickly swept off her feet and falls in love with him. Unfortunately, Luis has a jealous streak and a rather possessive personality, especially when it comes to women. He also has a prior girlfriend named Celia, (Yadira Jimenez). Celia also happens to share similar traits with Luis and is filled with jealous rage toward Amelia. So much so that she causes her rival in love to have an accident which leaves Amalia's face horribly scarred-so much so that she must now wear a mask in public. Filled with shame and fear of rejection, she decides to leave Luis without explanation or saying farewell, Her actions anger her former lover, who thinks she left him for better career opportunities. So, he decides to kill poor disfigured Amalia.

programme.title: A Tiny Audience
programme.desc:
A Tiny Audience is a music/talk-show recorded live in front of a "tiny audience" of dedicated fans in a very intimate setting. Musicians of all ages and genres perform completely organic, reaching the broadest possible audience. Musical artists get to speak about themselves, their songwriting, and their music. They get to show that they are real musicians, who can really perform their music without auto tune, tracks or choreography. The artists perform without rehearsal and go straight to the edge of their comfort zone. Most of the musicians have never performed this intimately in front of an audience they can look in the eyes, which is both intimidating and exhilarating. They are interviewed by hosts who are musicians themselves. The audience of fans, friends and family, management, and record labels gets closer to the artists, and the artists become more real than ever before. The result is a unique interaction between the musicians, hosts, and audience.
In each episode, the artists reveal things not know about them and their music. The shows open with a confession - something the audience will not know or believe about the artist. They then perform 3 songs: their biggest hit, the song they feel is their most important one, and a never-before performed acoustic version of a new song, a duet, or the cover they have always wanted to perform. Between songs, the artists discuss their creative process, important events in their lives, experiences, and their musical passion, and the audience (both present and at home) sees them like never before.

programme.desc: Empty pots lead to high tensions and low morale.

programme.title: Cheating Death

programme.title: Seeking the Catch
programme.desc: Mass amounts of stinging jellyfish in unfilled pots add insult to injury.

programme.title: MeatEater - Unconventional: Alaska Sooty Grouse Part 2
programme.desc: In Part One, Steve experienced the difficulty of hunting the Sooty grouse of southeast Alaska.

programme.title: MeatEater - Wild to Table: Memorable Meals Cooking Special
programme.desc: Steve loves to fish as much as he loves to hunt.Steve is on a river in SW Montana for a day to catch.

programme.title: MeatEater - Up at the Cabin: Prince of Wales Island Black Bear
programme.desc: Last spring, Steve decided to pass up an opportunity to kill a black bear in favor of an extended up close and personal experience. This time, Steve heads back to his shack on Prince of Wales Island and he's brought his friend Paul Neess from Vortex Optics along to share the experience of using a canoe to slip in close to these giant black bears.

programme.title: Bear Grease Roadshow - Folsom Points and Bear Hunting
programme.desc: In this episode, Clay Newcomb visits the Folsom archaeological site in Folsom, New Mexicowhere.

programme.title: Bear Grease Roadshow - Appalachian Bear Hunting with Hounds
programme.desc: In this episode, Clay explores the origins of the Plott Hound, a breed developed in the Southern Appalachian.

programme.title: Bear Grease Roadshow - Mountain Lion with Warner Glenn
programme.desc: Warner Glenn is an 87-year-old rancher and dry-ground mountain lion hunter in Arizona. In this episode, Clay Newcomb explores the story of the Glenns’ ranch, their hunting, and Warner’s incredible legacy. Warner is certainly one of the oldest living, working cowboys in America and isn’t showing any signs of slowing down as he and Clay hunt mountain lions.

programme.title: Cal in the Field - Rice Field Duck Hunting
programme.desc: Cal heads to a waterfowl mecca — California’s Sacramento Valley — where he joins Sean Doherty and local guide Scott Feist for a hunt in the heart of the nation’s largest agricultural region. The rice fields they’re hunting provide vital habitat for waterfowl outside of planting season. Thanks to ongoing conservation efforts, native ducks, geese, and shorebirds have returned to the area. The same water that grows food and attracts birds could also help revive the native fishery.

programme.title: Cal In the Field - Spearfishing for White Sea Bass
programme.desc: This week, Cal dives deep spearfishing for white sea bass with Joel Oelnik and Ryan Moore in Southern.

programme.title: Cal in the Field - Salmon vs Striper on California's Coastal Waterways
programme.desc: This week, Cal returns to Californias Sacramento Valleythis time diving below the surface of the Sacramento.

programme.title: Cal in the Field - Is CWD Changing Midwestern Deer Hunting?
programme.desc: This week, Cal visits Doug Durens farm outside Cazenovia, Wisconsin, to hunt deer and discuss Chronic Wasting.

programme.title: Exploring New Zealand’s Wild Flavors | DIRT New Zealand
programme.desc: They say you come to New Zealand as a stranger, but leave as family. In this episode of DIRT, the crew goes full circle—meeting Muttonbird chefs Emma and George on the South Island before hopping up to Raglan and working their way back down. Water-skiing next to snow-capped mountains, cooking in hot springs the Māori way, and stargazing at Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve are all on the itinerary. But it’s the final “Feed” done Kiwi-style that makes one thing clear—in this unique and stunning land, nobody’s a stranger for long.

programme.title: Tex-Mex Cuisine, Bull Riding & the Spirit of Texas | DIRT Texas
programme.desc: Anyone can be a cowboy—that’s the biggest thing the DIRT crew learned in Texas. From riding bulls in Fort Worth to off-roading in the Chihuahuan desert to forging their own path across this vast state with the help of local trailblazers Liz and Lou Lambert. Lucky for us, they even stopped by Huckberry’s own backyard in Austin for a quick ruck and a couple games of chicken sh*t bingo.

programme.title: Alaskan Fishing, Foraging, & Mountainside Cooking | DIRT Alaska
programme.desc: Hop in the float plane—a new episode of DIRT just dropped. Host Josh Rosen makes his way back to Alaska, “the land of air, sea, and sky,” for the first time since a family trip in ’93. All feast, no sleep is the name of the game as Josh makes the most of Alaska’s abundant and legendary daylight alongside local guides, fishermen, and even a couple grizzlies.

programme.title: Can You Surf & Eat Like a Local in Minnesota? | DIRT: The North
programme.desc: In Episode 3, Josh starts off in the Twin Cities where he meets up with Yia Vang, an award-winning Hmong chef and restaurant owner, bringing his family’s traditions and flavors to the American Midwest.

programme.title: Louisiana's Wild Food & Award-Winning Cuisine | DIRT New Orleans
programme.desc: In DIRT Episode II, New Orleans, Josh links up with Mason Hereford (owner of award-winning sandwich shop Turkey and the Wolf) and then hits the road for some epic hog hunting, swamp fishing, crawfish and oyster harvesting, and exploring Louisiana’s only water buffalo dairy farm. It all culminates in one incredible feast featuring unique regional dishes prepared by Mason with the help of some local friends.

programme.title: MeatEater - Southern Traditions: Virginia Doves
programme.desc: All across America hunters kick off the fall hunting season with a weekend of doves, friends, and food. Steve's lifelong friend, Ronny Boehme has invited Steve out to his digs in Virginia for the dove opener. To prep for opening day, Steve and Ron spend a day brushing up on their shooting skills on a sporting clays course followed by a pigeon hunt. Steve shares simple, delicious cooking methods for doves and pigeons.

programme.title: MeatEater - Mountain Meditation: BC Grizzly Bear
programme.desc: Ryan Callaghan and Aaron Evans join Steve on an epic adventure in British Columbia's Rockies to pursue grizzly bears. Steve has long desired to take a grizzly but only if he can do it in a way that fits his personal ethics. The hunt is dampened by frustrating weather but Steve finds a way to fix a tasty meal.
